title: MicroTaskJob primitive + Postgres job queue schema
goal: Land the durable MicroTaskJob primitive and Postgres queue schema that turns micro-task iteration into a first-class, auditable job loop. Folds WP-1-Micro-Task-Executor-v1 intent: MT definitions become typed rows with fresh-context-per-iteration, completion-signal parsing fields, bounded iteration limits, and 6-level escalation chain fields.
expected_diff_shape: New Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/process_ledger/micro_task_job.rs + src/backend/handshake_core/src/process_ledger/micro_task_queue.rs; new Postgres migration src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/NNN_micro_task_job_queue.sql; unit tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/micro_task_job_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: X.1 lease + router + repo merged so MicroTaskJob can bind to mailbox threads with proper claim semantics.
implementation_notes: Folded WP-1-Micro-Task-Executor-v1 intent in refinement.json line 995: 'Make micro-task iteration a first-class, auditable job loop with deterministic escalation and hard-gate pauses ... bounded iteration limits ... 6-level escalation chain (7B->7B-alt->13B->13B-alt->32B->HARD_GATE) with LoRA selection by task_tags.' Define `MicroTaskJob { job_id: Uuid v7, wp_id: String, mt_id: String, mt_contract_path: PathBuf, iteration_n: u32, max_iterations: u32, escalation_tier: EscalationTier (T7B|T7BAlt|T13B|T13BAlt|T32B|HardGate), escalation_history: Vec<EscalationStep>, task_tags: Vec<String>, lora_id: Option<String>, mailbox_thread_id: Option<Uuid v7> (links to X.1 thread for feedback/escalation), state: MicroTaskJobState (Queued|Claimed|Running|AwaitingVerification|Escalated|Completed|HardGated|Cancelled|Failed), claimed_by_session: Option<Uuid v7>, claimed_at_utc: Option, created_at_utc, updated_at_utc, completion_signal: Option<CompletionSignal>, progress_artifact_ref: Option<ArtifactPointer>, run_ledger_ref: Option<RunLedgerPointer> }`. Postgres table `kernel_micro_task_job` indexed on (state, escalation_tier, created_at_utc) and (wp_id, mt_id). Queue methods on `MicroTaskQueue`: `enqueue(job)`, `claim_next(executor_kind, capabilities) -> Option<MicroTaskJob>` (atomic SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED + UPDATE state=Claimed), `update_state(job_id, new_state, transition_reason)`, `escalate(job_id, new_tier, reason)` records an EscalationStep with timestamp + reason + predecessor_tier. The 6-tier escalation chain is canonicalized in `EscalationTier::next(current) -> Option<EscalationTier>` returning None at HardGate. Hard-gate transition triggers operator pause (forward-declared; MT-188 wires the escalation router). All Uuid v7. Tests: (a) enqueue+claim_next is atomic under 8 parallel claimers; (b) escalation chain progresses tier-by-tier, refuses to skip; (c) HardGate state blocks further claims; (d) cancellation transitions Running -> Cancelled cleanly.
risk_if_missed: Without a first-class MicroTaskJob primitive, MT iteration stays implicit chat replay (the folded stub's explicit anti-pattern); escalation becomes non-deterministic; distillation evidence (downstream MT-189) has no schema to attach to; silent infinite loops become possible.
validator_focus: Atomic claim under concurrency (SKIP LOCKED + FOR UPDATE pattern proven); escalation tier monotonic (cannot skip or downgrade except via explicit downgrade method); HardGate is terminal until operator releases via signed envelope.
acceptance_criteria_refs: PRESERVED_INTENT:WP-1-Micro-Task-Executor-v1 (first-class auditable job loop with deterministic escalation)

minimum_controls: SKIP LOCKED proven via 8-task parallel claim test asserting no double-claim.
Escalation tier monotonic enforced by CHECK constraint or trigger on state column.
HardGate release requires operator capability proof (forward-declared; MT-188 wires signed envelope check).
